ref: Hirah went back and told Judah, “I couldn't find the woman, and the people of Enaim said no prostitute had ever been there.”
text: Na ɔsanee baa Juda hɔ, na ɔbɛsee dɛ, Mennhu no; na eso ekuromfo no see dɛ, Edwaman biara mmbaa ha.
translated: And he came again to Judah and saw that he was there, and the men of the place said, No one comes here.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Na otuu no kwan fi Pomu afamu araa kodur Bɛtɛl, beebi mbrɛ ahyɛse no n’asesee wɔ mu no, wɔ Bɛtɛl nye Ai ntamu:
translated: And he arose and went from Beersheba, the well of the oath, for there was a well there, and went toward Haran, and stopped in Bethel on the way, between Bethel and Ai:

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Na Jakob bisaa no dɛ, Meserɛ wo, bɔ wo dzin kyerɛ me. Na ɔsee dɛ, Na iribisa me dzin ayɛ dɛn? Na banyin no hyiraa no wɔ hɔ.
translated: And Jacob asked him, "Tell me, what is your name?" And he said, "Why do you ask my name?" And he blessed him there.

ref: The clans of the Horites were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Ekunyin a wofi Horfo mu puei nye woyinom; ekunyin Lotan, Sobal, Zibeon, na Ana,
translated: These are the chiefs who came from Horite territory: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, and Anah,

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: Na onyinsenii, na ɔwoo babanyin: na ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn eyi m’ahorohoraa:
translated: And the wise man, he gave birth to a son: and he said, God has taken my shame away:

ref: God said, “I command the earth to produce all kinds of plants, including fruit trees and grain.” And that's what happened.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Asaase mfifir efuw, nhaban a wɔsow aba, na ndua a wɔsow nduaba dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tse, wɔ asaase do: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.
translated: And God saw that the earth was barren, there were no plants, and no trees had grown as they should on the earth: and He made them.

ref: I ask the Lord my God to bless Shem and make Canaan his slave.
text: Na ɔsee bio dɛ, Nhyira nka EWURADZE, Sem ne Nyankopɔn; Na wɔmma Keenan nyɛ n’akowaa.
translated: And again he said, Thanks be to GOD, the Almighty; And they made Keenan his helper.

ref: Each of Ishmael's sons was a tribal chief, and a village was named after each of them.
text: iyinom nye Ismael ne mba, na iyinom nye hɔn edzin, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn nkuroba, nye hɔn nseraban no tsetsee; etsitsir duebien dɛ mbrɛ hɔn aman tsetsee.
translated: They are the sons of Ishmael, and these are their names, by their villages, and by their encampments; twelve princes according to their nations.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Jakob frɛɛ bea no ne dzin Peniel; osiandɛ, ɔsee dɛ, Mehu Nyankopɔn mpu na mpu, naaso me kra enya nkwa.
translated: And Jacob called the name of the place Peniel; for, he said, I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.
text: Nna Laban kɔ rukotwitwa no nguan ho nhwi: na Rahel wiaa n’egya no suman.
translated: And Laban went to shear his sheep: and Rachel stole her father's idols.

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Na n’egya Isaak bisaa no dɛ, Woana nye wo? Na ɔsee no dɛ, Mara wo ba, w’abakan Esau a.
translated: And his father Isaac asked him, Who are you? And he said, I am your firstborn Esau.

ref: and he said, “Notice that all the rams are either spotted or speckled. I know everything Laban is doing to you,
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Afei ma w’enyiwa do, na hwɛ, mpɔnkyenyin a wɔfow mpɔnkyeber no nyinara hɔn ho yɛ nsensanee nsensanee, woranworan, nye ntokowa ntokowa: na mehu dza Laban reyɛ wo nyinara.
translated: And he said, Now behold, I have taken upon me to speak unto the Lord, and I said, Peradventure there shall be twenty found there. And he said, I will not destroy it for twenty's sake.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Nna akyɛdze no twaa mu dzii n’enyim: na ɔnoara dze, ɔtsenaa dɔm no mu anafua no. Jakob N’aperdzi Wɔ Peniel
translated: And he rose up that night, and took his two wives, and his two womenservants, and his eleven sons, and passed over the ford Jabbok.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Emi nye Bɛtɛl Nyankopɔn, beebi mbrɛ eseraa idum ho no a, beebi mbrɛ ebɔɔ me anohoba no a; afei soɛr, fi asaase yi do, na san kɔ w’awobea asaase do.
translated: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a pillar and where you made a vow to me. Now leave this land at once and go back to your native land.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Na Jakob see Laban dɛ, Fa me yer ma me, na me nda aso, ma monkɔ ne nkyɛn.
translated: And Jacob said to Laban, Give me my wife, for my days are fulfilled, that I may go in unto her.
